hero.headline:
Show your work.
Get paid.
Without risk.
hero.subline: You upload your website design, your client opens it via a link, comments directly on it, signs the contract and pays — the money is held securely by Pitchsite until you deliver.

problem.label: The problem
problem.headline: You delivered. The client went silent.
problem.body:
Three weeks of work. The design is done, the client says "looks great". You deliver the finished website. And then: no reply. No feedback. No payment.

No contract, no protection, no recourse. This happens to web designers every single day.
problem.caption: No protection without a contract.

solution.label: The solution
solution.headline: Everything you need before you hand over a single file.

items.title: Your client sees the design — and comments directly on it
items.body: You upload your HTML file or ZIP package. Pitchsite generates an access code. Your client enters this code on pitchsite.de and sees your design in the browser — no app, no account. They can click anywhere and leave a comment. You see the feedback in real time in your dashboard.

items.title: Contract and payment in one step
items.body: When the client wants to buy, they click "Accept project". They see the full scope of work, the delivery date, and the price — everything you entered when creating the pitch. They create an account, sign the legally binding purchase contract digitally, and pay by credit card or direct debit.

items.title: The money sits with Pitchsite until you deliver
items.body: After payment, the money sits in the Pitchsite account — not with the client, not in your account. You work on the finished website. When you're done, you upload it and the client approves. Only then does Pitchsite automatically transfer your amount. Nobody can disappear.

items.title: Subscription and retainer models, fully protected
items.body: Offering monthly payments? You decide how many installments — for example, the first three — are held securely through Pitchsite before you start building the final product. This means you're fully protected even on subscription and retainer projects, without having to demand full upfront payment.

howitworks.label: How it works
howitworks.headline: Four steps. From design to payment.

steps.title: Upload your design
steps.body: Upload your HTML file or ZIP package to Pitchsite. Enter the price, scope of work, and delivery date. Pitchsite generates a 10-digit access code — send it to your client via WhatsApp, email, or SMS.

steps.title: Client opens the design & gives feedback
steps.body: Your client enters the code on pitchsite.de. They see your design directly in the browser — no download, no app. They can click anywhere and leave a comment. You reply directly in your dashboard.

steps.title: Sign the contract & pay
steps.body: When the client wants to proceed, they click "Accept project". They see the full contract with scope, price, and delivery date, sign digitally, and pay. The money is then held securely by Pitchsite.

steps.title: Deliver & get paid
steps.body: You deliver the finished website. The client reviews and approves. Pitchsite transfers your amount automatically. If the client doesn't respond, approval is deemed granted after 14 days — legally protected.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.headline: Transparent. No hidden fees.
pricing.subline: Pitchsite only earns when you earn — a commission on processed deals, nothing else.
pricing.provisionNote: Commission example: On a €3,000 project (Pro plan) → €150 commission, payout €2,850
pricing.stripeNote: Plus Stripe payment fees (~1.4% + €0.25 credit card · ~0.4% + €0.25 SEPA). Shown transparently before checkout.

waitlist.label: Waitlist
waitlist.headline: Be there when it launches.
waitlist.subline: Exactly one email when Pitchsite goes live. No newsletter, no spam.
waitlist.consent: I have read the privacy policy and agree to it.
waitlist.successSub: We sent you a confirmation. Click the link in it — done.
waitlist.errorExists: This email is already on the waitlist.
waitlist.errorInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
waitlist.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

transparency.title: How does Pitchsite make money?
transparency.metaDescription: A transparent explanation of Pitchsite's business model: commission, subscription prices, and how payment processing works.
transparency.intro: Pitchsite makes money in exactly one way: a commission on successfully processed deals. No hidden fees, no bloated subscriptions with features locked behind paywalls.

sections.title: The commission
sections.body:
When a deal is processed through Pitchsite — contract signed, payment received, approval granted — Pitchsite retains a commission from the invoice amount. This commission is 5% on the Pro plan and 3% on the Agency plan.

The freelancer sees their payout amount when creating the pitch, before activating anything. No surprises.

sections.title: Example calculation

examples.label: Monthly payment (subscription)
examples.hint: Commission is calculated once on the combined sum of the first 12 monthly payments — not on each individual installment.

sections.footnote: Agency plan (3% commission): On a €3,000 project → €90 commission → payout €2,910

sections.title: Payment processing
sections.body:
All payment processing runs through Stripe — a globally licensed payment institution. The money sits in the Pitchsite Stripe account until approval is granted. Pitchsite is not a payment institution and requires no BaFin license for this: Stripe takes on the regulatory responsibility.

The terms of service explicitly state: "Payment processing is handled exclusively by Stripe Payments Europe Ltd., a licensed payment institution."

sections.title: The subscription
sections.body: The subscription model (Free / Pro / Agency) covers operating costs: servers, storage, email, support. Prices will be announced at launch. Anyone on the waitlist gets the early bird price — guaranteed.
sections.note: A small portion of the subscription fee is secure profit for Pitchsite — regardless of how many deals close in a given month. This is intentional: it keeps the commission low, gives Pitchsite financial predictability, and ensures the product keeps improving even in quieter months.

items.q: Is the contract legally valid — including in Austria and Switzerland?
items.a: Yes. Pitchsite is designed for the entire DACH region. The digitally signed contract meets the requirements of German BGB, Austrian ABGB, and Swiss OR — enforceable in courts across all three countries. Payments are supported in EUR and CHF.

items.q: What happens if the client refuses to approve delivery?
items.a: The client must name specific defects that deviate from the agreed scope of work. General dissatisfaction isn't legally sufficient. If they don't respond within 14 days, approval is automatically granted — that's stipulated in the contract. For genuine disputes, Pitchsite mediates based on the written scope of work.

items.q: Are Stripe fees on top of the Pitchsite commission?
items.a: Yes — Stripe charges approximately 1.4% + €0.25 (EU credit card) or 0.4% + €0.25 (SEPA direct debit) on top of the Pitchsite commission. These are shown to you transparently before finalizing, so you can price your projects correctly from the start. Pitchsite earns from the commission — not from hidden payment fees.

items.q: What if I miss the agreed delivery date?
items.a: The delivery date is part of the contract — but not a trap. If you need more time, you can propose a new deadline to the client via Pitchsite. They need to agree, and the money stays secured in the meantime. Arbitrary payment refusals due to minor delays are ruled out.

items.q: Where is my data stored? Is it GDPR-compliant?
items.a: All data is stored on servers in the EU (Frankfurt). Fully GDPR-compliant — a data processing agreement (DPA) is available on request. Payment data is processed exclusively by Stripe; Pitchsite never sees or stores credit card data.

items.q: Can I submit Webflow or Figma designs?
items.a: Yes. Webflow projects can be uploaded as a ZIP export — Pitchsite automatically resolves all assets. Figma designs can be embedded directly via share link. Both work without additional software or plugins.

confirmed.headline: Confirmed.
confirmed.body: You're on the list. We'll reach out exactly once — when Pitchsite goes live.
